description: Prototype policy for deterministic energy routing: use local solar first, reserve forecast solar for the battery, route EV solar before the deadline, sell remaining solar, and buy grid energy only for uncovered load or EV deadline completion.

priority_order.reason: Serve non-shiftable household load before export or charging.
Honor the forecast battery reservation after household load is served.
Use EV-usable solar surplus when the EV is plugged in and the charging target has a deadline.
Export solar only after household, battery, and EV solar allocations are satisfied.
Buy grid electricity for any household load not covered by solar.
Buy the cheapest fixture-backed grid energy needed to complete the EV target before departure.

limitations: All routing decisions use synthetic prototype fixtures.
The layer emits advice-only allocations; it does not send live device commands.
Battery routing follows the forecast reservation and does not model intra-day battery cycling.
Solar opportunity cost is the missed feed-in credit from the tariff fixture.
